items.title: Ebola-hit DR Congo faces 'catastrophic collision' of disease and conflict, WHO warns
items.summary: Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus said fighting in DR Congo was hampering efforts to stop spread

items.title: Israeli strike in Gaza City kills new head of Hamas's military wing
items.summary: At least three people were killed in the attack, which came despite a ceasefire with Hamas being in place.

items.title: Five people found alive after week trapped in flooded Laos cave
items.summary: The search is continuing for a further two villagers who are still missing, rescuers say.

items.title: Dozens killed in Lebanon as Israel intensifies strikes
items.summary: Israel says it struck 100 Hezbollah infrastructure sites and fighters in Lebanon, after PM Benjamin Netanyahu vows to "crush" the Iran-backed group.

items.title: Woman caught after 30 years on the run is jailed for armed robberies
items.summary: Klette was finally caught in a flat in Berlin in 2024 after more than 30 years on the run and put on trial last year.

items.title: One killed and nine missing after chemical explosion at US paper mill
items.summary: Nine others were injured and another nine are missing after a tank ruptured at a paper mill in Longview, Washington.

items.title: Hotel that refused to give tourist tap water acted lawfully, Italian court rules
items.summary: The Italian Supreme Court rejected a tourist's claim that her consumer rights were breached when she was only offered €7 bottled mineral water.

items.title: Trump-backed challenger beats veteran US senator in Texas primary
items.summary: The result sets up what will be a closely watched contest in November with Paxton's Democratic opponent.

items.title: Iran condemns US strikes as 'gross violation' of ceasefire
items.summary: The attacks took place while Iranian and Qatari negotiators were in Doha for peace talks.

items.title: YouTuber arrested for allegedly using AI to defame Korean actor
items.summary: The YouTuber allegedly made false claims that fuelled a career-ending scandal for Kim Soo-hyun.

items.title: First Ghanaians set to be repatriated from South Africa over anti-immigrant protests
items.summary: It follows recent protests that have led to fears there could be a resurgence in xenophobic violence.

items.title: South Korea detains dissident who fled China in rubber boat
items.summary: Dong Guangping, a police officer turned activist, reached Korean waters after 30 hours at sea.

items.title: Nasa unveils next steps to build permanent Moon base
items.summary: Nasa plans to send hopping drones and roving vehicles to the Moon as part of plans for a permanent Moon base.

items.title: Major film union in India calls for boycott of superstar Ranveer Singh
items.summary: Singh is facing industry backlash over his decision to exit from the upcoming Bollywood film Don 3.

items.title: Hundreds of children die within months as measles cases soar in Bangladesh
items.summary: The country has recorded more than 60,000 suspected cases of measles in just over two months.

items.title: Russia 'relentlessly targeting' critical infrastructure and democracy, GCHQ says
items.summary: The spy agency's head will set out threats facing the UK and the measures she believes need to be taken to confront them on Wednesday.

items.title: Four killed as school minibus collides with train in Belgium
items.summary: Two schoolchildren, their chaperone and the minibus driver were killed in the crash, says a Belgian minister.

items.title: Can EU find a Russia whisperer to mediate an end to war in Ukraine?
items.summary: With the US pulling out of trilateral talks with Russia and Ukraine, the EU is looking for potential candidates to step in.

items.title: How Trump is transforming Washington - by adding his face everywhere
items.summary: Sarah Smith visits places across the US capital where the president has hung banners, renamed a site or is rebuilding something.

items.title: India's communists once ruled millions. What happened to them?
items.summary: The story of the rise and decline of India’s communists - from ruling states to struggling for relevance.

items.title: Why temperature records are being not only broken but smashed
items.summary: The combined effects of a heat dome and climate change have brought extreme warmth to western Europe.

items.title: Wave of child abuse cases shakes schools in Paris
items.summary: A school assistant goes on trial accused of sexual mistreatment of children in his care, in the latest case to rock the Paris school system.

items.title: Watch: UFC arena construction begins at White House ahead of fight
items.summary: The massive structure will host a cage fight as part of celebrations marking the 250th anniversary of the US Declaration of Independence.

items.title: Paris 'punishingly hot' as Western Europe hit by heatwave
items.summary: Hugh Schofield reports from Paris as a heat dome settled over Western Europe bringing temperatures above the average for May.

items.title: Watch: Bridge collapses and car swept away in China river
items.summary: The driver and passengers of the car managed to escape before the bridge gave way in Xiaogan.

items.title: Watch: Drones crash into water after Sydney light show malfunction
items.summary: Almost 90 drones fell from the sky over Sydney's Darling Harbour during a popular winter light show.
